data.title: HIVE Digital Capacity Crosses 10 EH/s in May, Aims to More Than Double That by Year-End
data.description:
Bitcoin miner HIVE Digital Technologies (HIVE) has surpassed 10 exahash per second (EH/s) in hashrate capacity, a 58% increase from April, driven by the launch of a 100-megawatt hydro-powered site in Paraguay. The company said in a press release on Friday that it's on track to reach 25 EH/s by the end of 2025.The firm mined 139 bitcoin in May, or an average rate of 4.5 BTC per day
source: https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2025/06/06/hive-digital-capacity-crosses-10-ehs-in-may-aims-to-more-than-double-that-by-year-end

data.title: Has Bitcoin been captured by politics and institutions?
data.description:
Bitcoin is now front and center at political conferences and corporate boardrooms. In this episode of Byte-Sized Insight, we're at Bitcoin 2025 exploring whether it has been captured, or simply arrived.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/has-bitcoin-been-captured-by-politics-institutions?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to monitoring by on-chain analyst Yu Jin, James Wynn's on-chain address has been cleared, and the remaining assets have all entered CEX, a total of 1.91 million USDC, of ​​which 1.5 million were transferred to Kucoin, 335,000 to MEXC, and 75,000 to Gate.

data.title: Fed's June Meeting Deemed Insignificant Amid Tailored Job Report
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, JPMorgan's Chief Economist Mike Feroli stated that the May employment report was almost "tailor-made" for the Federal Reserve. This report supports the Fed's current inclination to maintain its stance and patiently observe developments in employment and inflation before deciding on future policy directions.

As a result, the June Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting is considered an "insignificant event."

data.title: Lens founder: The infrastructure supported by Lens V2 is about to end, and it is necessary to migrate to V3 as soon as possible
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Lens founder Stani.eth posted on the X platform that since Lens V3 has been widely adopted throughout the ecosystem, the Lens V2 support infrastructure will be terminated. If you have not yet migrated to Lens V3, you need to complete it as soon as possible.

data.title: Fed's Harker: Rate cut still possible later this year
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Fed's Harker said that the deficit must be controlled at a time when the US financial system faces growing challenges, and he is "very worried" about the current government fiscal situation. Harker also said: "We are becoming increasingly blind in terms of key data. We are worried that the quality of economic data is declining. Uncertainty makes it very difficult to predict the outlook for monetary policy. But amid uncertainty, the Fed may still cut interest rates later this year." (Jinshi)

data.title: Philadelphia Fed President Harker Expresses Concerns Over U.S. Fiscal Deficit
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Philadelphia Federal Reserve President Patrick Harker has expressed significant concerns about the U.S. fiscal deficit amid growing challenges in the financial system. Harker emphasized the need for deficit control and voiced his worries about the current government's fiscal situation.

Harker also highlighted issues with key economic data, noting a decline in data quality and increasing uncertainty. This uncertainty complicates the forecasting of monetary policy. Despite these challenges, Harker indicated that the Federal Reserve might still consider an interest rate cut later this year.

data.title: Opinion: The bull market may come when Coinbase app returns to the top ten of Apple Store
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News James Wynn wrote on the X platform that if the Coinbase app is among the top ten of Apple's Apple Store again, the crypto market may be close to the top of the cycle. For example, during the market rally in November 2024, the app soared from 428th to 8th in the US App Store in one week.
